operations.value: Small randomized crossover studies in adults dissatisfied with their sleep found that 3 g of glycine taken shortly before bedtime improved some subjective ratings of sleep quality and next-morning refreshment compared with placebo.
operations.notes: Direct human evidence, conservatively framed around small crossover studies and subjective outcomes.

sources.title: Subjective effects of glycine ingestion before bedtime on sleep quality
Glycine ingestion improves subjective sleep quality in human volunteers, correlating with polysomnographic changes

notes: Adds the best-supported direct sleep claim.
